$comment: Structured mirror of the features in site/repo-data.js. This file is documentation only; the page does not load it. When a feature changes, update repo-data.js (the page's content source) and this file in the same PR. The version and AgentProof score fields below mirror site/repo-data.js meta, which the sync workflow maintains; feature copy is updated by hand since it involves editorial decisions.

features.title: Small, test-backed changes
features.summary: Each proposed change is tightly scoped and fenced by a test, and the CI ratchet keeps every assertion intact. Reviews stay quick and the intent is easy to read.

features.title: Separate roles for trust
features.summary: The maker, the checker, and the merge authority are always different roles, so each change gets a genuine second look before it lands.

features.title: Anti-gaming ratchet in CI
features.summary: A check running in your pipeline keeps tests, types, and coverage honest across JavaScript, Python, Java, and .NET.

features.title: Adopts your setup on day one
features.summary: It follows your existing CI, code owners, and branch rules. There is no new process for the team to learn.

features.title: Improves as it goes
features.summary: Real corrections become staged lessons. Once an owner promotes them, they become durable rules: the loop gets smarter with each cycle.

features.title: Language-aware across your stack
features.summary: The anti-gaming ratchet is language-aware across JavaScript, Python, Java, and .NET today. Broader enterprise estates are on the roadmap.

features.title: Operator control panel
features.summary: A dashboard shows gate status, governance events, and the levers an owner uses to arm, pause, and review autonomy, so control stays in plain sight.

features.title: Verifiable repo context
features.summary: A Merkle-verified snapshot gives each agent a compact, tamper-evident map of the repository, so its context is current and trustworthy without rereading the whole tree.

features.title: Compliance evidence on demand
features.summary: An MCP server exposes attestation and evidence tools, so a host repository can produce a signed record of what ran and what held.
